# Exclude auto-generated docs from document-changes skill

Source: [prefecthq/prefect#21275](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/prefect/pull/21275)

This task is a **markdown_authoring** task. The repository's agent-instruction file(s)
need to be updated. Read the existing content and add or modify the rules so that
the file matches the intent described below.

## Files to update

- `.claude/skills/document-changes/SKILL.md`

## What to add / change

Fixes an issue where the automated docs update workflow modified `docs/v3/api-ref/settings-ref.mdx`, which is auto-generated by `scripts/generate_settings_ref.py`. See #21272.

The `document-changes` skill already had a guideline at the bottom saying to skip `v3/api-ref/`, but the instruction was buried in the "Important guidelines" section and was not followed during CI automation. This PR promotes the exclusion into the workflow steps themselves so it's encountered during sequential execution rather than only as an afterthought.

**Changes:**
- Added an explicit **"Exclude auto-generated files"** block in Step 3 (Map changes to existing docs) listing specific paths and detection heuristics
- Added an exclusion bullet as the first item in the Edit mode (CI) checklist in Step 4
- Expanded the existing bottom-of-file guideline with specific file examples (`settings-ref.mdx`) and stronger language

### Checklist

- [x] This pull request references any related issue by including "closes `<link to issue>`"
  - References #21272 (the automated PR that triggered this fix)
- [ ] If this pull request adds new functionality, it includes unit tests that cover the changes
  - N/A — this is a prompt/instruction change to a Claude Code skill file
- [ ] If this pull request removes docs files, it includes redirect settings in `mint.json`.
- [ ] If this pull request adds functions or classes, it includes helpful docstrings.

## Acceptance

The grader runs `pytest /tests/test_outputs.py` which checks that distinctive
literal strings from the intended update are present in the target file(s).
You do not need to write any code outside of the markdown file(s).
